summary: Implemented ADR 0202 on the workstream branch, added the repo-managed Excalidraw runtime plus generated diagram workflow, converged draw.example.com end to end through targeted Hetzner DNS publication and the shared authenticated NGINX edge, and verified the frontend, collaboration room, patched collaboration origin, socket routing, and TLS-authenticated public entrypoint.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python -m pytest tests/test_excalidraw_runtime_role.py tests/test_generate_diagrams.py tests/test_nginx_edge_publication_role.py tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py tests/test_lv3_cli.py tests/test_uptime_contract.py -q` passed with `71 passed in 1.90s`.

`uvx --from pyyaml python scripts/generate_diagrams.py --check` returned `Architecture diagrams OK`, and `make syntax-check-excalidraw` passed after the playbook gained the targeted DNS-publication prelude.

`uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` returned `Repository data models OK` after adding the missing `excalidraw` redundancy catalog entry.

`make converge-excalidraw` completed successfully after the runtime switched from host networking to explicit loopback port publishing, the BusyBox-incompatible `grep --fixed-strings` usage was replaced with `grep -F`, and the playbook added targeted DNS provisioning for `draw.example.com`.

A post-apply ad hoc check on `docker-runtime-lv3` returned `frontend_ok` for `http://127.0.0.1:3095/` and `room_ok` for `http://127.0.0.1:3096/`, with the collaboration room health banner still containing `collaboration server is up`.

A live asset grep inside `excalidraw-app` found `https://draw.example.com` in `/usr/share/nginx/html/assets/index-D8FNeBTZ.js`, confirming the runtime bootstrap patched the upstream public collab origin to the private deployment.
`nginx -T` on `nginx-lv3` included `location ^~ /socket.io/ {`, confirming the new generic prefix-route template path is active for the Excalidraw room backend.

verification.check: Public DNS and authenticated TLS entrypoint
verification.observed: `dig +short draw.example.com @8.8.8.8` returned `203.0.113.1`, and `curl -Ik --resolve draw.example.com:443:203.0.113.1 https://draw.example.com` returned `HTTP/2 302` to `https://draw.example.com/oauth2/sign_in?rd=https://draw.example.com/` with the expected hardened response headers.

`./scripts/validate_repo.sh generated-docs` was exercised on the workstream branch and still requested the integration-owned canonical-truth rewrite for top-level `README.md`; that exception is documented and intentionally deferred to the merge-to-main step per branch protection rules.

notes: During the first live converge attempt, the frontend container never opened `127.0.0.1:3095` because the upstream image still listened on its internal default port while the compose file used `network_mode: host`; the final implementation publishes explicit loopback host ports instead.

The initial DNS-publication attempt used the broad `hetzner_dns_records` sync pattern and failed on unrelated zone drift; the final playbook uses a targeted single-subdomain converge for `draw.example.com` so the workflow only mutates the record this ADR owns.

At receipt time Google Public DNS already served `draw.example.com`, while Cloudflare's `1.1.1.1` had not yet caught up. The runbook now documents `dig @8.8.8.8` plus `curl --resolve` for immediate post-create verification.
Merge to `main` still needs the protected integration-file step only: update `VERSION`, `changelog.md`, top-level `README.md`, and `versions/stack.yaml` after branch integration.
